title: Sum of Squares & Variance Demo
text:
#| '!! shinylive warning !!': |
#|   shinylive does not work in self-contained HTML documents.
#|   Please set `embed-resources: false` in your metadata.
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 900

from shiny import App, render, ui, reactive
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# 1. Define the User Interface
app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.layout_sidebar(
        ui.sidebar(
            ui.input_select(
                "dist", "Base Distribution Shape",
                {"norm": "Normal (Symmetric, Bell)", 
                 "unif": "Uniform (Flat, Rectangular)", 
                 "exp": "Exponential (Right-Skewed)",
                 "bimodal": "Bimodal (Two Peaks)",
                 "beta": "Beta (Left-Skewed)"}
            ),
            # These inputs are now always visible and apply to ALL distributions
            ui.input_numeric("pop_mu", "Set Population Mean (μ):", value=0.0, step=1.0),
            ui.input_numeric("pop_var", "Set Population Variance (σ²):", value=1.0, min=0.1, step=0.5),
            ui.hr(),
            ui.input_action_button("draw_1", "Add 1 Score", class_="btn-primary"),
            ui.input_action_button("draw_10", "Add 10 Scores", class_="btn-success"),
            ui.input_action_button("reset", "Start Over", class_="btn-danger"),
            ui.markdown("Compare calculations using the **Population Mean (μ)** vs. the **Sample Mean (M)**."),
            width=300
        ),
        ui.output_plot("main_plot", height="800px")
    )
)

# 2. Define the Server Logic
def server(input, output, session):
    sample_data = reactive.Value([])

    # Mathematical properties of the raw, unscaled base distributions
    def get_base_params(d):
        if d == "norm":      return 0.0, 1.0
        elif d == "unif":    return 0.5, np.sqrt(1.0 / 12.0)
        elif d == "exp":     return 1.0, 1.0
        elif d == "bimodal": return 0.0, np.sqrt(4.25)
        elif d == "beta":    return 0.8, np.sqrt(16.0 / 1100.0)

    # Generate raw scores, then standardize and scale them to match user inputs
    def draw_scaled_scores(n_scores):
        d = input.dist()
        target_mu = float(input.pop_mu())
        target_sigma = np.sqrt(float(input.pop_var()))
        base_mu, base_sigma = get_base_params(d)
        
        # 1. Draw from raw distribution
        if d == "norm":      raw = np.random.standard_normal(n_scores)
        elif d == "unif":    raw = np.random.uniform(size=n_scores)
        elif d == "exp":     raw = np.random.exponential(size=n_scores)
        elif d == "bimodal": 
            centers = np.where(np.random.rand(n_scores) < 0.5, -2, 2)
            raw = np.random.normal(centers, 0.5)
        elif d == "beta":    raw = np.random.beta(8, 2, size=n_scores)
            
        # 2. Scale and shift to match user's mu and sigma
        scaled_scores = (raw - base_mu) * (target_sigma / base_sigma) + target_mu
        return scaled_scores.tolist()

    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.draw_1)
    def add_one():
        sample_data.set(sample_data() + draw_scaled_scores(1))

    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.draw_10)
    def add_ten():
        sample_data.set(sample_data() + draw_scaled_scores(10))

    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.reset, input.dist, input.pop_mu, input.pop_var) 
    def clear_data():
        sample_data.set([])

    # 3. Render the Plots
    @output
    @render.plot
    def main_plot():
        data = sample_data()
        n = len(data)
        d = input.dist()
        
        target_mu = float(input.pop_mu())
        true_var = float(input.pop_var())
        target_sigma = np.sqrt(true_var)
        base_mu, base_sigma = get_base_params(d)
        
        fig, (ax1, ax2, ax3) = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(10, 10))
        fig.tight_layout(pad=5.0)
        
        # --- TOP PLOT: Population Distribution ---
        # Dynamically frame the x-axis for ALL distributions based on sigma
        x_vals = np.linspace(target_mu - 4*target_sigma, target_mu + 4*target_sigma, 1000)
        
        # To draw the scaled PDF, we reverse-map the x-axis back to the base distribution
        scale_factor = target_sigma / base_sigma
        x_base = (x_vals - target_mu) / scale_factor + base_mu
        
        if d == "norm":   
            y_base = (1 / np.sqrt(2 * np.pi)) * np.exp(-0.5 * x_base**2)
        elif d == "unif": 
            y_base = np.where((x_base >= 0) & (x_base <= 1), 1, 0)
        elif d == "exp":  
            y_base = np.where(x_base >= 0, np.exp(-x_base), 0)
        elif d == "bimodal":
            y_base = (1 / np.sqrt(2 * np.pi)) * (np.exp(-2 * (x_base + 2)**2) + np.exp(-2 * (x_base - 2)**2))
        elif d == "beta":
            y_base = np.where((x_base >= 0) & (x_base <= 1), 72 * (x_base**7) * (1 - x_base), 0)
            
        # Scale the height of the PDF so the area underneath remains 1
        y_vals = y_base / scale_factor
            
        ax1.plot(x_vals, y_vals, color='gray', linewidth=2)
        ax1.fill_between(x_vals, y_vals, alpha=0.2, color='gray')
        ax1.axvline(target_mu, color='black', linestyle='--', label=f'Population mean $μ$ ({target_mu:.2f})')
        ax1.set_xlim(target_mu - 4*target_sigma, target_mu + 4*target_sigma)
        
        if n > 0:
            ax1.scatter(data, np.zeros(n), color='dodgerblue', zorder=5, label='Sample Points', alpha=0.6)
            ax1.axvline(np.mean(data), color='red', linestyle='-', label=f'Sample mean $M$ ({np.mean(data):.2f})')
            
        ax1.set_title("Population Distribution & Sample Scores")
        ax1.legend(loc='upper right')
        
        # Base state before any data is drawn for bottom two plots
        if n == 0:
            for ax in [ax2, ax3]:
                ax.set_xlim(0, 10)
                ax.set_ylim(0, max(5, true_var * 2))
                ax.text(5, max(5, true_var * 2) / 2, "Click 'Add 1 Score' to begin", ha='center', va='center', fontsize=12)
            ax2.set_title("Sum of Squares Growth")
            ax3.set_title("Variance Estimates")
            return fig
            
        # --- Calculate Histories ---
        n_values = np.arange(1, n + 1)
        ss_mu_vals, ss_xbar_vals, diff_ss_vals = [], [], []
        var_mu_vals, var_xbar_vals, diff_var_vals = [], [], []
        
        for i in range(1, n + 1):
            subset = np.array(data[:i])
            xbar = np.mean(subset)
            
            # Sum of Squares
            ss_mu = np.sum((subset - target_mu)**2)
            ss_xbar = np.sum((subset - xbar)**2)
            diff_ss = i * (target_mu - xbar)**2 
            
            ss_mu_vals.append(ss_mu)
            ss_xbar_vals.append(ss_xbar)
            diff_ss_vals.append(diff_ss)
            
            # Variance
            var_mu = ss_mu / i
            var_xbar = ss_xbar / (i - 1) if i > 1 else np.nan
            diff_var = (target_mu - xbar)**2
            
            var_mu_vals.append(var_mu)
            var_xbar_vals.append(var_xbar)
            diff_var_vals.append(diff_var)
                
        # --- MIDDLE PLOT: Sum of Squares ---
        ax2.plot(n_values, ss_mu_vals, color='purple', marker='o', markersize=4, label='SS using $\\sum (x_i - μ)^2$')
        ax2.plot(n_values, ss_xbar_vals, color='darkorange', marker='s', markersize=4, label='SS using $\\sum (x_i - M)^2$')
        ax2.plot(n_values, diff_ss_vals, color='seagreen', marker='^', markersize=4, label='$n(μ - M)^2$')
        
        ax2.set_title("Sum of Squares (SS) vs. Sample Size")
        ax2.set_xlabel("Sample Size (n)")
        ax2.set_ylabel("Sum of Squares")
        ax2.set_xlim(0, max(10, n + 1))
        ax2.legend(loc='upper left')
        
        # --- BOTTOM PLOT: Variance ---
        ax3.axhline(true_var, color='black', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True σ² ({true_var:.3f})')
        ax3.plot(n_values, var_mu_vals, color='purple', marker='o', markersize=4, alpha=0.7, label='$s^2$ using $\\frac{\\sum (x_i - \\mu)^2}{n}$')
        if n > 1:
            ax3.plot(n_values, var_xbar_vals, color='darkorange', marker='s', markersize=4, alpha=0.7, label='$s^2$ using $\\frac{\\sum (x_i - M)^2}{n-1}$')
        ax3.plot(n_values, diff_ss_vals, color='seagreen', marker='^', markersize=4, alpha=0.7, label='$n(μ - M)^2$')
            
        ax3.set_title("Variance vs. Sample Size")
        ax3.set_xlabel("Sample Size (n)")
        ax3.set_ylabel("Variance")
        ax3.set_xlim(0, max(10, n + 1))
        
        # Dynamic y-axis for variance
        max_var = max(max(var_mu_vals), np.nanmax(var_xbar_vals) if n > 1 else 0, max(diff_ss_vals), true_var)
        ax3.set_ylim(0, max_var * 1.5)
        ax3.legend(loc='upper right')
        
        return fig

app = App(app_ui, server)

title: Multiple Samples & Sampling Distributions Demo
text:
#| '!! shinylive warning !!': |
#|   shinylive does not work in self-contained HTML documents.
#|   Please set `embed-resources: false` in your metadata.
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 950

from shiny import App, render, ui, reactive
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# 1. Define the User Interface
app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.layout_sidebar(
        ui.sidebar(
            ui.input_select(
                "dist", "Base Distribution Shape",
                {"norm": "Normal (Symmetric, Bell)", 
                 "unif": "Uniform (Flat, Rectangular)",
                 "exp": "Exponential (Right-Skewed)",
                 "bimodal": "Bimodal (Two Peaks)",
                 "beta": "Beta (Left-Skewed)"}
            ),
            ui.input_numeric("pop_mu", "Set Population Mean (μ):", value=0.0, step=1.0),
            ui.input_numeric("pop_var", "Set Population Variance (σ²):", value=1.0, min=0.1, step=0.5),
            ui.input_numeric("n", "Sample Size (n):", value=30, min=2, step=1),
            ui.hr(),
            ui.input_action_button("draw_1", "Draw 1 Sample", class_="btn-primary"),
            ui.input_action_button("draw_10", "Draw 10 Samples", class_="btn-success"),
            ui.input_action_button("draw_100", "Draw 100 Samples", class_="btn-warning"),
            ui.input_action_button("reset", "Clear History", class_="btn-danger"),
            ui.markdown("Watch how the sampling distributions build as you draw more samples of size $n$."),
            width=300
        ),
        ui.output_plot("main_plot", height="850px")
    )
)

# 2. Define the Server Logic
def server(input, output, session):
    history = reactive.Value({
        'means': [], 'ss_mu': [], 'ss_xbar': [], 'diff_ss': [], 
        'var_mu': [], 'var_xbar': [], 'diff_var': []
    })

    def get_base_params(d):
        if d == "norm":      return 0.0, 1.0
        elif d == "unif":    return 0.5, np.sqrt(1.0 / 12.0)
        elif d == "exp":     return 1.0, 1.0
        elif d == "bimodal": return 0.0, np.sqrt(4.25)
        elif d == "beta":    return 0.8, np.sqrt(16.0 / 1100.0)

    def draw_k_samples(k):
        d = input.dist()
        target_mu = float(input.pop_mu())
        target_sigma = np.sqrt(float(input.pop_var()))
        base_mu, base_sigma = get_base_params(d)
        n = int(input.n())
        
        size = (k, n)
        
        if d == "norm":      raw = np.random.standard_normal(size)
        elif d == "unif":    raw = np.random.uniform(size=size)
        elif d == "exp":     raw = np.random.exponential(size=size)
        elif d == "bimodal": 
            centers = np.where(np.random.rand(k, n) < 0.5, -2, 2)
            raw = np.random.normal(centers, 0.5)
        elif d == "beta":    raw = np.random.beta(8, 2, size=size)
            
        scaled_scores = (raw - base_mu) * (target_sigma / base_sigma) + target_mu
        means = np.mean(scaled_scores, axis=1)
        
        # Sum of Squares
        ss_mu = np.sum((scaled_scores - target_mu)**2, axis=1)
        ss_xbar = np.sum((scaled_scores - means[:, None])**2, axis=1)
        diff_ss = n * (target_mu - means)**2
        
        # Variances (Mean Squares)
        var_mu = ss_mu / n
        var_xbar = ss_xbar / (n - 1) if n > 1 else np.zeros(k) * np.nan
        diff_var = n * (target_mu - means)**2 # Updated to match n(μ - M)^2
        
        curr = history.get().copy()
        curr['means'].extend(means.tolist())
        curr['ss_mu'].extend(ss_mu.tolist())
        curr['ss_xbar'].extend(ss_xbar.tolist())
        curr['diff_ss'].extend(diff_ss.tolist())
        curr['var_mu'].extend(var_mu.tolist())
        curr['var_xbar'].extend(var_xbar.tolist())
        curr['diff_var'].extend(diff_var.tolist())
        history.set(curr)

    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.draw_1)
    def add_1(): draw_k_samples(1)

    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.draw_10)
    def add_10(): draw_k_samples(10)

    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.draw_100)
    def add_100(): draw_k_samples(100)

    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.reset, input.dist, input.pop_mu, input.pop_var, input.n) 
    def clear_data():
        history.set({'means': [], 'ss_mu': [], 'ss_xbar': [], 'diff_ss': [], 'var_mu': [], 'var_xbar': [], 'diff_var': []})

    # 3. Render the Plots
    @output
    @render.plot
    def main_plot():
        curr = history.get()
        num_samples = len(curr['means'])
        
        d = input.dist()
        target_mu = float(input.pop_mu())
        true_var = float(input.pop_var())
        target_sigma = np.sqrt(true_var)
        base_mu, base_sigma = get_base_params(d)
        n = int(input.n())
        
        fig, axs = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))
        fig.tight_layout(pad=6.0)
        ax1, ax2, ax3, ax4 = axs.flatten()
        
        # --- TOP LEFT: Population Distribution ---
        x_vals = np.linspace(target_mu - 4*target_sigma, target_mu + 4*target_sigma, 1000)
        scale_factor = target_sigma / base_sigma
        x_base = (x_vals - target_mu) / scale_factor + base_mu
        
        if d == "norm":   
            y_base = (1 / np.sqrt(2 * np.pi)) * np.exp(-0.5 * x_base**2)
        elif d == "unif": 
            y_base = np.where((x_base >= 0) & (x_base <= 1), 1, 0)
        elif d == "exp":  
            y_base = np.where(x_base >= 0, np.exp(-x_base), 0)
        elif d == "bimodal":
            y_base = (1 / np.sqrt(2 * np.pi)) * (np.exp(-2 * (x_base + 2)**2) + np.exp(-2 * (x_base - 2)**2))
        elif d == "beta":
            y_base = np.where((x_base >= 0) & (x_base <= 1), 72 * (x_base**7) * (1 - x_base), 0)
            
        y_vals = y_base / scale_factor
            
        ax1.plot(x_vals, y_vals, color='gray', linewidth=2)
        ax1.fill_between(x_vals, y_vals, alpha=0.2, color='gray')
        ax1.axvline(target_mu, color='black', linestyle='--', label=f'True μ ({target_mu:.2f})')
        ax1.set_xlim(target_mu - 4*target_sigma, target_mu + 4*target_sigma)
        ax1.set_title("Population Distribution")
        ax1.set_xlabel("Score Value")
        ax1.legend(loc='upper right')
        
        if num_samples == 0:
            for ax, title in zip([ax2, ax3, ax4], ["Sampling Distribution of Mean (M)", "Sampling Distribution of Sum of Squares", "Sampling Distribution of Variance (Mean Squares)"]):
                ax.set_xlim(0, 10)
                ax.set_ylim(0, 5)
                ax.text(5, 2.5, f"Click 'Draw Samples' to build distribution\n(n = {n})", ha='center', va='center', fontsize=11)
                ax.set_title(title)
            return fig

        # --- TOP RIGHT: Sampling Distribution of the Mean ---
        # Plotted as density to overlay theoretical curve
        ax2.hist(curr['means'], bins=20, density=True, color='dodgerblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.6)
        
        # Theoretical Distribution Overlay
        std_err = target_sigma / np.sqrt(n)
        x_m = np.linspace(target_mu - 4*std_err, target_mu + 4*std_err, 200)
        y_m = (1 / (std_err * np.sqrt(2 * np.pi))) * np.exp(-0.5 * ((x_m - target_mu) / std_err)**2)
        ax2.plot(x_m, y_m, color='darkgreen', linewidth=2, label='Theoretical $N(μ, σ^2/n)$')
        
        ax2.axvline(target_mu, color='black', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True μ ({target_mu:.2f})')
        ax2.axvline(np.mean(curr['means']), color='red', linestyle='-', linewidth=2, label=f'Mean of M\'s ({np.mean(curr["means"]):.2f})')
        ax2.set_title(f"Sampling Dist. of the Mean (Total Samples: {num_samples})")
        ax2.set_xlabel("Sample Mean (M)")
        ax2.legend(loc='upper right')

        # --- BOTTOM LEFT: Sampling Distribution of SS ---
        ax3.hist(curr['ss_mu'], bins=20, color='purple', alpha=0.5, label='SS using $\\sum (x_i - μ)^2$')
        ax3.hist(curr['ss_xbar'], bins=20, color='darkorange', alpha=0.5, label='SS using $\\sum (x_i - M)^2$')
        ax3.hist(curr['diff_ss'], bins=100, color='seagreen', alpha=0.6, label='$n(μ - M)^2$')
        
        # Add ticks for means
        ax3.axvline(np.mean(curr['ss_mu']), color='purple', linestyle='dashed', linewidth=2.5)
        ax3.axvline(np.mean(curr['ss_xbar']), color='darkorange', linestyle='dashed', linewidth=2.5)
        ax3.axvline(np.mean(curr['diff_ss']), color='seagreen', linestyle='dashed', linewidth=2.5)
        
        ax3.set_title("Sampling Dist. of Sum of Squares (SS)")
        ax3.set_xlabel("Sum of Squares")
        ax3.legend(loc='upper right')

        # --- BOTTOM RIGHT: Sampling Distribution of Variances (Mean Squares) ---
        ax4.hist(curr['var_mu'], bins=20, color='purple', alpha=0.5, label='$s^2$ using $\\frac{\\sum (x_i - \\mu)^2}{n}$')
        if n > 1:
            ax4.hist(curr['var_xbar'], bins=20, color='darkorange', alpha=0.5, label='$s^2$ using $\\frac{\\sum (x_i - M)^2}{n-1}$')
        ax4.hist(curr['diff_var'], bins=100, color='seagreen', alpha=0.6, label='$n(μ - M)^2$')
        ax4.axvline(true_var, color='black', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'True σ² ({true_var:.3f})')
        
        # Add ticks for means
        ax4.axvline(np.mean(curr['var_mu']), color='purple', linestyle='dashed', linewidth=2.5)
        if n > 1:
            ax4.axvline(np.nanmean(curr['var_xbar']), color='darkorange', linestyle='dashed', linewidth=2.5)
        ax4.axvline(np.mean(curr['diff_var']), color='seagreen', linestyle='dashed', linewidth=2.5)
        
        ax4.set_title("Sampling Dist. of Variance (Mean Squares)")
        ax4.set_xlabel("Variance Estimate")
        ax4.legend(loc='upper right')

        return fig

app = App(app_ui, server)

title: Interactive Statistics Demonstrations
text:
Welcome! These interactive modules are designed to help you build a visual, intuitive understanding of some of the most fundamental (and sometimes tricky) concepts in statistics.
Instead of just memorizing formulas, you are going to generate real data, watch how the math reacts, and see the geometric proofs of these concepts unfold in real-time.

Choose a Module to Begin:
1. Single Sample Growth

What you will do: Draw individual scores one at a time from a population of your choice.
What you will learn: Watch how the Sum of Squares (\(SS\)) grows as your sample size increases. More importantly, you will see exactly why calculating variance using the sample mean (\(M\)) systematically underestimates the true variance compared to using the population mean (\(\mu\))—and why we have to divide by \(n-1\) to fix it!

2. Sampling Distributions

What you will do: Draw dozens (or hundreds!) of samples all at once.
What you will learn: See the Central Limit Theorem in action as the distribution of sample means forms a bell curve. You will also track the average of all your sample variances to prove that dividing by \(n-1\) creates an unbiased estimator that perfectly targets the true population variance (\(\sigma^2\)).







Note



These modules are designed to be exploratory and interactive. Feel free to experiment with different population parameters, sample sizes, and numbers of samples to see how the concepts hold up under various conditions. The goal is to develop a deep, intuitive understanding of these statistical principles, not just to see them work in one specific case.
